summary: R2 fixes QA REQUEST_CHANGES by making the compact task rail first-screen actionable at 1280x720 and 1440x900, replacing task rail appointment rejection implementation jargon with player-facing eligibility language, and adding Playwright regressions for viewport intersection and en/zh jargon absence.
commit: 0da728e3; no commit made, review the working tree diff
requested_action: Review R2 fixes for M7-TASK-RAIL-COLLAPSED-DRAWERS-001 against QA REQUEST_CHANGES findings and acceptance criteria.

qa_findings_response.finding: P1 first-screen task rail and drawer actions were offscreen at 1280x720 and 1440x900.
qa_findings_response.response: Fixed. TaskRail now renders before DistrictPanel in the side rail. The side rail is ordered before the map at the <=1320px desktop breakpoint, and side/task drawer content uses bounded internal scrolling so long inspector and route queue content cannot push the rail below the first viewport.
qa_findings_response.evidence: New Playwright regression `M7 task rail is first-screen actionable at required desktop viewports` asserts all six task rail cards, the active obligations drawer, and the drawer primary action intersect the viewport at 1280x720 and 1440x900.

qa_findings_response.finding: P1 player copy leaked `read-model reasons` / `只读模型原因` in `shell.taskRail.appointments.reasonRejected`.
qa_findings_response.response: Fixed. The English copy now says `The selected candidate is blocked by office eligibility concerns.` and the Chinese copy now uses player-facing office-eligibility language without read-model/internal terminology.
qa_findings_response.evidence: New Playwright regression `M7 task rail appointment-error copy hides implementation jargon in English and Chinese` checks the appointment-error task rail copy in en-US and zh-CN and asserts no read-model/raw/internal jargon appears in the task rail.

qa_findings_response.finding: P2 test coverage did not fail when the task rail was below the fold or when implementation jargon was visible.
qa_findings_response.response: Fixed. Added viewport-intersection helper and two dedicated e2e regressions for default first-screen actionability and en/zh jargon absence. Updated the web shell unit test to match the new required DOM order while preserving decision assistant internal ordering checks.
qa_findings_response.evidence: `pnpm test:e2e` now runs 25 tests and passed 25/25.

tests_run.result: Working tree has modified R2 files only: apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.test.tsx, apps/web/src/styles/shell.css, packages/ui/src/i18n.ts, packages/ui/src/index.tsx, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts. No commit made.
Task list loaded; M7-TASK-RAIL-COLLAPSED-DRAWERS-001 remains IN_PROGRESS for client_engineer.
Command completed with no ready-task output.
Vite production build passed. Existing chunk-size warnings for lib/main chunks over 500 kB remain.
Playwright passed: 25/25 tests, including new 1280x720 and 1440x900 task rail viewport regressions and en/zh jargon checks.
Passed format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Bottom panels default to compact task rail cards for obligations, appointments, succession, campaign prep, notifications, and results.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Six task rail cards remain present and are now asserted to intersect the default viewport at 1280x720 and 1440x900.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Only the currently relevant drawer expands; other areas preserve map context and do not swamp the first screen.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Only the active obligations drawer renders by default. The rail appears before long inspector details, and long side/drawer content is bounded with internal scrolling.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Task rail cards state one problem, one reason, one action, and severity/urgency using text, icon/shape, and color.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Existing card structure remains intact; rejected appointment reason now uses player-facing office eligibility language in both locales.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Responsive coverage includes 1280x720 and 1440x900 without text overlap or hidden primary actions.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: New Playwright test asserts task rail cards, active drawer, and drawer primary action intersect the viewport at 1280x720 and 1440x900 and that document-level horizontal overflow is absent.

decisions: Preserved existing read-model and GameCommand/query boundaries; no sim-core, protocol, save, content-source, docs/GATE, roadmap, CI, server, telemetry, cloud-save, or manual node battle scope was touched.
Used layout order and bounded scrolling rather than duplicating domain state or creating a separate first-screen data source.
Scoped negative jargon assertions to the player-facing task rail, because existing debug/developer and map read-model strings outside this task remain separate surfaces.
risks: QA should visually confirm the new side-first ordering at 1280x720 because the map moves below the rail at that breakpoint to satisfy first-screen actionability.
The route queue remains available inside the obligations drawer with internal scroll behavior; QA should confirm the reduced visible area still supports scanning.
Build still reports pre-existing chunk-size warnings over 500 kB; this task did not address bundling.
